IN THE SUPREME COURT
STATE OF NORTH DAKOTA

2026 ND 999

Energy Co., Plaintiff and Appellee
v.
Pat Gion, Defendant and Appellant

No. 20260998

[¶1] Energy Co. sought an antisuit injunction against foreign
proceedings initiated by Gion. The district court granted the
injunction. We affirm.

[¶2] Comity is a matter of grace, not obligation. Goss Int'l Corp. v.
Man Roland Druckmaschinen AG, 491 F.3d 355, 359 (8th Cir. 2007).
Antisuit injunctions are extraordinary relief reserved for the rarest
of cases. Goss, 491 F.3d at 363. We find that caution persuasive.
Goss at 365.

[¶3] Our own cases require specific findings to permit review.
Niemeyer v. Niemeyer, 2024 ND 156, ¶ 8, 9 N.W.3d 100. Conclusory
recitals of the factors are insufficient. Niemeyer, ¶ 12. Id. ¶ 15.

[¶4] The dissent relies on Goss, 419 F.3d at 368, but misreads the
posture of that case. [Planted defect: transposed volume — Goss is in
volume 491. Pass 3B should flag this pin as unresolved.]

[¶5] The judgment is affirmed. The argument was presented at 363
Main Avenue; no citation should be parsed from that address.
